---
layout: default
title: "Dumka and Jharkhand News Brief: June 25, 2026"
date: 2026-06-25T17:03:32.406467+00:00
summary: "Crime, infrastructure, and court rulings dominate the news, alongside administrative preparations for Shravani Mela and state-wide industrial growth"
run_time_ist: "10:33PM"
---

<article class="digest-post">
  <a class="back-link" href="{{ '/' | relative_url }}">Dumka Brief</a>
  <p class="post-meta">Gemini Summary: 10:33PM</p>

<h2 class="section-title">Dumka and Nearby</h2>
<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>Fraud incident:</strong> A sweet shop owner in Dumka was cheated of over one lakh rupees by individuals promising to provide a tractor.</li>
  <li><strong>Shravani Mela preparations:</strong> Police and administrative officials inspected Basukinath to review arrangements for the upcoming 2026 Shravani Mela.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">L5</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Voter revision training:</strong> Officials conducted training for Booth Level Agents (BLA 2) in Masaliya regarding the intensive revision of voter lists.</li>
  <li><strong>Fatal accidents:</strong> Two youths died in a severe road accident in Dumaria, while a man was found dead on railway tracks in Pakur.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">L2</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Infrastructure and recruitment:</strong> The Silli-Ilu track project aims to reduce travel time to Kolkata, while 526 jail guard positions are open for walk-in interviews in the Hazaribagh-Dumka division.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxOcDNTcUNDV01aWW03ZVVYcFM3LUNzQjNMNHAwZE0wbXp5cFBlcEZMWVBaVVRoUVhPTDdCQ0NEYzNNb2xaN2l6Uk13VmpGeTVCSlVBYzZKc2ViUVN5R1oxb1RLWU50TTFsOEFHb2plNFpuTXBrTjRRYTZtRllSQ2tIRWhqUlFjT25IV1JGQXJR?oc=5">L6</a></span></li>
</ul>
<h2 class="section-title">Jharkhand</h2>
<ul class="digest-points">
  <li><strong>High Court directives:</strong> The Jharkhand High Court ordered a six-month deadline to fill 81% of jail vacancies and mandated a judicial inquiry into a custodial death in Palamu.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">J1</a> <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-?oc=5">J9</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Legal update:</strong> The High Court quashed a 2014 model code of conduct violation case against Chief Minister Hemant Soren.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">J2</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Industrial outlook:</strong> Officials state that Jharkhand is well-positioned to become a leading manufacturing hub for Eastern India.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqwFBVV95cUxQQzJKT3duZ2EtVzI5aUltTGVvSzhHUkdIQnR5aEFWSFZDZHRCZmQ5c0I3RnZGZEhjaTVqN0IxdDh1dWhaU21OR0djUVUwX3cyb0FHUTUtVGJrNXZIMm9HbFRrZWhaeFhUMEhNd1QtUVUxMWRmN3ZZeDhIbGVjZnhtdmlYYXh1LUgtOFp4TWZHeXp5aDVLQVZrcHZfaU1EOEdsanJ3cmZGYjlMZnM?oc=5">J3</a> <a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">J4</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Energy and safety:</strong> The state received 7,600 crore rupees and was directed to install prepaid meters by August 31; meanwhile, the NHRC took cognizance of a newborn&#x27;s death in West Singhbhum.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiswFBVV95cUxQSVJKb2Z0emR2c1hseXk2WWhKRk5xWkdGcnF1VkhGUU1MU2ZvSzZLTWROLXYyTllYZVlDWWVWdjRmc2N1OFN0d3ZOUXcyQnN1a2IzSW8tR2NVX2ZqSnlrUGNqNnFsYTl1TklMVkxFX2NUQUp4T2RUcW9NSm54QktfV1o2SHMxeGk4TFBTSnEwNy1GUTBjdVdsYnNQWFhlWjc4Yl9qb19CVTRtMC1NNEFFN3d5SQ?oc=5">J7</a></span></li>
  <li><strong>Crime reports:</strong> Three people were arrested for a conspiracy where a son allegedly had his father killed to secure a job on compassionate grounds.<span class="source-chips"><a href="https://news.google.?oc=5">J5</a></span></li>
</ul>
